evals.name: natural-language-deadline-parsing
evals.prompt: I want to set up an AAE call about our PCI DSS 4.0 transition strategy. I need to have direction by next Friday — we have a steering committee meeting and I'd like to present a recommendation backed by faculty input. Please submit via IANS when ready.
evals.expected_output: Submit mode (default). Skill calls ians_whoami(), passes entitlement gate, recommends Phone resolution. parse_urgency.py interprets 'next Friday' as the upcoming Friday relative to the session date. Sets expedite_request: true with deadline ISO. Surfaces the warning that a short window is tighter than the standard 8-12 business day Phone turnaround. Skill should acknowledge the ambiguity of 'next Friday' (two common interpretations) and confirm the user's intent before locking the draft.
evals.notes: Edge case: 'next Friday' has two common interpretations. The parser picks the upcoming one; the skill surfaces ambiguity via warning + confirm step.
structural_checklist: Phone resolution recommended
expedite_request set to true
Deadline parsed to ISO date
Deadline >= today (passes minDate validation)
Standard turnaround timing flagged given tight deadline
Skill confirms 'next Friday' interpretation with user

evals.name: ambiguous-guidance-asks-user
evals.prompt: I'm curious about emerging trends in cybersecurity insurance — what should I be paying attention to over the next 12 months? Set up an AAE for me.
evals.expected_output: Skill recommends Phone resolution (open-ended trend question). Drafts Driver and Question. infer_guidance returns empty array (purely informational, no strategic/exec or technical/tactical signal). Skill ASKS the user to pick Strategic or Technical (or both) — does not silently default to Strategic. Lists the two options with platform form's helper text.
evals.notes: This tests the ambiguity branch — the inference script returns empty array and the skill must surface that as an explicit user prompt rather than fabricating guidance.
structural_checklist: Phone resolution recommended
Skill asks user to pick guidance type
Both Strategic and Technical options shown
No silent default applied
Required-field enforcement present

evals.name: over-length-input-trimming
evals.prompt: [Context: 4000-character monologue about a multi-year SOC modernization journey, with details about team size, tooling history, regulatory environment, prior decisions, vendor relationships, board dynamics, and 6 distinct questions for faculty.] Set up an AAE call.
evals.expected_output: Skill drafts payload. Driver is trimmed to <=1000 chars (specifics over background). Details is trimmed to <=500 chars. Question is trimmed to 3-5 items (not all 6). Skill surfaces what was trimmed in the review step — 'I trimmed your Q3 vendor history from Details to fit the 500-char cap; let me know if any of it should go back in.' User can request specific items be added back.
evals.notes: draft_payload.py trims with a sentence/word boundary; that's intentional. The skill's job at review time is to be transparent about the trimming.
structural_checklist: Driver respects 1000-char cap
Details respects 500-char cap
Question is 3-5 items (not 6+)
trimmed_fields surfaced in review step
User offered the option to restore specific content

evals.name: urgency-no-rush-overrides-cues
evals.prompt: [Programmatic test for parse_urgency.py.] User says 'no rush — whenever you can fit me in over the next few weeks works fine.' Verify expedite_request stays false even though 'next few weeks' has a duration cue.
evals.expected_output: parse_urgency returns expedite_request:false, deadline:null, matched_phrase contains 'no rush', reasoning notes the explicit deflation. The 'no rush' signal must override duration cues so users can downplay urgency in their own words.
evals.notes: Sprint 2 unit-flavor test — checks that no_rush language wins over relative duration phrases.
structural_checklist: expedite_request is false
deadline is null
matched_phrase contains 'no rush' or equivalent
Reasoning explains the override

evals.name: scheduling-mixed-routes-correctly
evals.prompt: [Programmatic test for parse_scheduling.py.] User says 'I'm in EST, mornings work better, here's my Calendly: https://calendly.com/javier-rivera'. Verify the URL routes to calendarlink and the time-zone/availability text routes to availability.
evals.expected_output: parse_scheduling returns calendarlink containing the Calendly URL, availability containing 'EST' and 'mornings'. extracted_urls includes the Calendly URL. Both fields respect their character caps. trimmed_fields is empty for short input.
evals.notes: Sprint 2 unit-flavor test for the routing logic.
structural_checklist: calendarlink contains the Calendly URL
availability contains time-zone and preference text
extracted_urls is non-empty
Neither field exceeds its cap

evals.name: connector-unavailable-graceful-failure
evals.prompt: I want to submit an Ask-an-Expert about our PCI DSS 4.0 audit prep. We're a payments processor. Send it now via the IANS MCP, please.
evals.expected_output: Skill calls ians_whoami(). Submit mode is the default. Skill builds the form-shaped review, user confirms, validate_submission.py passes, submit_via_connector.py returns status:connector_unavailable (ians_request_aae not registered or server_error). Skill surfaces the error inline and offers three options verbatim: Try again, Contact Client Services, Save as a scope draft. No JSON artifact is produced. Save as scope draft shows the Step 1 scope-deferral message. Skill does not fabricate success.
evals.notes: DAAS-194. Replaces the former Option-B artifact fallback eval.
structural_checklist: ians_whoami called
Entitlement gate passed
validate_submission.py invoked before submit
submit_via_connector.py invoked with payload
Response status is connector_unavailable
Three graceful-failure options surfaced verbatim
No JSON artifact produced
No silent continuation or fabricated case ID

evals.name: submit-mode-option-a-mocked
evals.prompt: [Programmatic test for the future Option A path.] Given a fully-populated AAE payload and a mocked successful connector response (case_id 00012345, tracking_url, expected_response_window), verify that submit_via_connector.py returns status:submitted with the case ID surfaced.
evals.expected_output: submit_via_connector.py invoked with --mock-response pointing at a success-shaped JSON. Script returns status:submitted, case_id:'00012345', tracking_url, expected_response_window matching the resolution. idempotency_key is generated. Skill surfaces case ID and tracking URL to the user with the appropriate timeline language.
evals.notes: Tests the connector path the skill takes once ians_request_aae ships. Uses IANS_REQUEST_AAE_AVAILABLE=1 + --mock-response.
structural_checklist: submit_via_connector exits 0
Response status is 'submitted'
case_id present and matches mock
tracking_url present
expected_response_window populated
idempotency_key generated

evals.name: edge-connector-down-refuses-both-modes
evals.prompt: Request an AAE about our zero-trust rollout.
evals.expected_output: Skill calls ians_whoami(). Connector is unavailable (network error, auth error, or tool-not-found). Skill stops at Step 0 and shows the connector-required message verbatim. Does NOT proceed to drafting — submit flow requires a connected MCP server because the skill can't verify identity, entitlements, or origin without it.
evals.notes: Run with the IANS MCP connector disconnected. Tests Step 0's hard gate.
structural_checklist: Skill stops at Step 0
Connector-required message shown verbatim
No drafting performed
No file produced
No submission attempted without connector

evals.name: connector-payload-canonical-shape
evals.prompt: [Programmatic test for submit_via_connector.build_connector_payload.] Given a fully-populated Phone payload with list-shaped question and short-form guidance, verify the connector input uses canonical question string and canonical guidance array.
evals.expected_output: build_connector_payload emits question as canonical '1. Q\n2. Q' string with sub-labels stripped, and guidance as ['Strategic / Executive', 'Technical / Tactical']. idempotency_key is passed through.
evals.notes: Replaces the artifact-shape eval. Covered by evals/tests/test_submit_via_connector.py.
structural_checklist: question serialized to canonical '1. Q\n2. Q' form
guidance normalized to canonical Salesforce picklist labels
idempotency_key present on connector payload

evals.name: submit-error-mapping
evals.prompt: [Programmatic test for submit_via_connector.py error path.] Mock the connector returning error_code=validation_failed with details {driver: 'exceeds limit'}. Verify the script returns retryable:true with a user-friendly message and preserves the per-field details.
evals.expected_output: submit_via_connector.py with --mock-response pointing at a validation-error-shaped mock returns status:error, error_code:validation_failed, retryable:true, details preserved, user_message that's actionable. Skill response would route the user back to Step 4 (review/edit) rather than retrying blindly.
evals.notes: Sprint 3 unit-flavor test. Catches regressions in error mapping or message phrasing.
structural_checklist: Status is 'error'
error_code matches input
retryable is true for validation_failed
details preserved with per-field info
user_message is non-empty and actionable

evals.name: question-serializer-canonical-shape
evals.prompt: [Programmatic test for validate_submission.py + submit_via_connector.build_connector_payload.] Given a payload whose question field is a list ['Cyberattacks — How exposed are we?', 'Energy & Supply Chain: How do we prioritize?', 'Reporting — How do we frame the risk?'], verify canonical question serialization and that validate_submission.py counts three questions.
evals.expected_output: canonicalize_questions accepts the list and emits '1. How exposed are we?\n2. How do we prioritize?\n3. How do we frame the risk?' — leading em-dash and colon category labels removed, items renumbered. validate_submission.py accepts both the list shape and the pre-serialized string via the same canonical path; the question count is 3 (passes the Phone 3-5 range). fixtures/sample-payload-v040-canonical.json exercises the list shape end-to-end.
evals.notes: DAAS-167. Covered programmatically by evals/tests/test_aae_common.py, test_submit_via_connector.py, and test_validate_submission.py.
structural_checklist: build_connector_payload accepts list[str] or a pre-serialized string and emits canonical '1. Q\n2. Q\n3. Q'
Em-dash and colon sub-labels are stripped from the start of each question
validate_submission.py accepts both shapes via the canonical payload schema
fixtures/sample-payload-v040-canonical.json exercises the list shape

evals.name: guidance-canonical-picklist-values
evals.prompt: [Programmatic test for infer_guidance.py + validate_submission.py + submit_via_connector.build_connector_payload.] Verify guidance is emitted as the canonical Salesforce picklist labels 'Strategic / Executive' and 'Technical / Tactical', and that short-form aliases are accepted but normalized.
evals.expected_output: infer_guidance.py returns ['Strategic / Executive'] / ['Technical / Tactical'] / both (never the short form). validate_submission.py accepts short-form aliases ('Strategic') as valid but emits a guidance 'normalized' warning instructing the skill to rewrite to canonical. build_connector_payload writes the canonical array form. Confirmed the SFDC picklist values match exactly (spaces around the slash).
evals.notes: DAAS-168. SFDC picklists are strict; mismatched values silently drop the field. Covered by evals/tests/test_infer_guidance.py and test_validate_submission.py.
structural_checklist: infer_guidance.py returns the canonical strings
validate_submission.py accepts short-form aliases on input but normalizes and warns
build_connector_payload writes the canonical form
SFDC picklist values match exactly: 'Strategic / Executive', 'Technical / Tactical'

evals.name: placeholder-unfilled-blocks-submission
evals.prompt: [Programmatic test for validate_submission.py.] Submit a payload where the Questions field still contains the literal placeholder '[needs your input — what 3-5 specific questions...]' (or is empty). Verify validation blocks before submit.
evals.expected_output: validate_submission.py returns valid:false with a 'placeholder_unfilled' error on the question field. The same code fires for a placeholder driver, a placeholder guidance value, and a placeholder deadline when expedited. Empty required fields are still rejected (missing). List-form question fields are checked item by item, not just the container. Exit code is 1.
evals.notes: DAAS-169. Covered by evals/tests/test_validate_submission.py PlaceholderUnfilledTest.
structural_checklist: validate_submission.py adds a placeholder_unfilled error code
Validation rejects any required field containing the literal placeholder string
Validation rejects empty required fields
List-form fields check each item, not just the container

evals.name: no-partial-reviews-all-sections-render
evals.prompt: I want to submit an Ask-an-Expert about our cloud IAM consolidation. Set it up as a Phone call.
evals.expected_output: Skill recommends Phone. The FIRST review message renders every required section for Phone/Undecided in a single message — Resolution Type, Driver, Specific Questions, Other Details, Guidance, Expedite + Deadline (with turnaround windows), Email (read-only), Scheduling, Submitter. Other Details and Scheduling appear on the first pass with [optional] placeholders even though nothing was extracted; the user is not forced to prompt for them. A Faculty Poll run instead skips Other Details, Guidance, and Scheduling entirely.
evals.notes: DAAS-170. Reported by Kate Lombard — Scheduling never surfaced and Other Details only appeared after a follow-up prompt.
structural_checklist: Step 4 enforces no-partial-reviews — required section list is locked per resolution
Scheduling renders for Phone/Undecided with [optional] placeholders when parse_scheduling returns null
First review message includes every required section (eval fails if any is missing)
Faculty Poll path skips Other Details, Guidance, and Scheduling entirely

evals.name: active-incident-no-improvised-ir-or-service-names
evals.prompt: We're in the middle of an active breach right now — attackers are still in our environment. I want to set up an Ask-an-Expert.
evals.expected_output: Step 0.5 detects active-incident language ('in the middle of', 'active breach', 'still in'). The skill proceeds straight to the AAE flow — drafts the submission, presents the form-shaped review, submits. It does NOT improvise incident-response advice or 'while we wait, here's what you can do' parallel-work suggestions, and it does NOT name or recommend any other IANS service (ICC, Incident Command Center, retainers). Behavior is identical on Opus and Sonnet.
evals.notes: DAAS-171. Observed in claude.ai Chat 1 (Iran war scenario). ICC referrals are a coordinator/account-manager call.
structural_checklist: Active-incident language detected (Step 0.5)
No parallel-work suggestions offered
No IANS service named beyond AAE
Out-of-scope list documents the rule
Holds for both Opus and Sonnet

evals.name: ians-search-grounding-before-drafting
evals.prompt: Set up an AAE about how peers are handling SEC cyber disclosure rules.
evals.expected_output: Before drafting Driver/Questions from open-web knowledge, the skill calls ians_search with the topic phrase (Step 2.5). When results exist it surfaces up to 3 candidate IANS sources as linked titles and asks the user before continuing; selected sources land in context.related_ians_content and open-web becomes supplementary. When ians_search returns no results, the skill says so plainly and continues from conversation context only. When ians_search is not registered, the skill skips grounding silently and proceeds — it never blocks the AAE flow on search availability.
evals.notes: DAAS-173. In Cowork Chat 4 the skill cited open-web sources despite IANS coverage.
structural_checklist: Step 2.5 documented in SKILL.md with exact user-facing wording
Skill calls ians_search before any open-web drafting
When results exist, up to 3 candidates surfaced and user asked before continuing
When no results, skill states so and continues from conversation context only
Documented behavior when ians_search MCP tool is not registered

evals.name: connector-submit-runs-validation-gate
evals.prompt: [Beta connector active.] Trigger the AAE skill in submit mode with a thin prompt (driver only, no questions stated). Proceed through review without filling the Questions field and say 'submit'.
evals.expected_output: Step 4.5 runs validate_submission.py on the connector submit path. The empty/placeholder Questions field is rejected (placeholder_unfilled / missing) and the skill re-prompts for 3-5 questions (1-3 for Faculty Poll) before any ians_request_aae call. The submission is blocked until valid. As defense in depth the connector itself rejects an unfilled-placeholder or blank question server-side, but the skill must not rely on that. Linked to DAAS-169 (same root rule, different surface).
evals.notes: DAAS-172. Observed against the Beta connector. Client-side gate covered by evals/tests/test_validate_submission.py; server-side guard covered by ians-mcp test/tools/request-aae.test.js.
structural_checklist: Validation gate (Step 4.5) runs on the connector submit path before ians_request_aae
placeholder_unfilled and empty-field rejections block the ians_request_aae call
Verified end-to-end against the Beta connector with the thin-prompt repro
Linked to the placeholder-survives bug as related (same root rule, different surface)

evals.name: questions-always-must-ask
evals.prompt: Submit an AAE — here's the gist: we need help with our zero-trust rollout sequencing and which controls to prioritize first.
evals.expected_output: Step 3 treats Specific Questions as must_ask on every run. The placeholder + prompt render above any Claude-drafted questions, and the draft renders inside a 'suggested starting point' callout — clearly a suggestion. Each question shows a ({len}/{cap}) counter, with a section total below the list. The counter reflects the locked payload value, not just typed input. The field does not lock until the user explicitly confirms or rewrites the questions; saying 'submit' without acknowledging the questions re-prompts for confirmation.
evals.notes: DAAS-174. Questions in the user's own words get better faculty answers; a silently-accepted draft traces back to Claude, not the user.
structural_checklist: Step 3 SKILL.md spec documents always-must_ask for Questions
Each question renders with a ({len}/{cap}) counter
Section total renders below the list
Counter reflects the locked payload value, not just typed input
Field does not lock without explicit user confirmation

evals.name: aae-recommendation-includes-tied-rationale
evals.prompt: [Chained-skill scenario] Another Ask IANS Skill hits the limit of unsupervised synthesis on a post-breach litigation-prep question and recommends an AAE.
evals.expected_output: Whenever an AAE is recommended — self-triggered or chained — the skill leads with a one-line rationale tied to the specific signal in the conversation, in the format 'Faculty review fits here because {signal} — {what a faculty member adds that the AI can't}.' Generic 'you might want to talk to an expert' framing fails. The rationale ties to a concrete conversational signal (e.g., post-breach litigation exposure), not a generic value-prop.
evals.notes: DAAS-175. Applies to self-triggered and chained invocations.
structural_checklist: SKILL.md documents the always-rationale rule with the format and 2-3 examples
Rule applies to self-triggered and chained invocations
Rationale ties to a specific signal in the conversation, not a generic value-prop
Skill never recommends an AAE without a tied rationale

evals.name: connector-default-no-website-redirect
evals.prompt: [Beta connector active + AAE entitlement.] Ask the skill to submit an AAE about board reporting metrics.
evals.expected_output: With the IANS MCP connected and entitlement present, the skill drafts the AAE, presents the form-shaped review, and submits via the connector after confirmation. It does NOT direct the user to the AAE form on iansresearch.com to fill out manually. When the connector is unavailable, the skill surfaces graceful failure options — not a website redirect and not a JSON artifact. The Beta connector is recognized as available in Step 0.
evals.notes: DAAS-182. Reported by Kate Lombard (Cowork Chat #3). Submit via connector is the default; website redirect is not a path this skill offers when the connector is available.
structural_checklist: Connector + entitlement detection in Step 0 recognizes the Beta connector
Default path is connector submission, not website redirect
Website redirect never fires; connector_unavailable graceful failure when the tool cannot submit
Review/edit/enhance affordance preserved before submit

evals.name: post-submit-references-client-services
evals.prompt: [Post-submit message check.] Complete an AAE submission and read the confirmation.
evals.expected_output: The post-submit confirmation references IANS Client Services as the contact point: coordinator contacts the client within 24-48 hours to schedule. Faculty turnaround (4-6 business days Faculty Poll, 8-12 Phone/Undecided) is presented as a separate downstream window — not conflated with first CS contact. It does NOT say the user will hear back from faculty. The same correction holds across the connector-success message and the close (Step 6).
evals.notes: DAAS-183. Reported by Kate Lombard. Faculty produce the deliverable but IANS Client Services coordinates the response loop.
structural_checklist: Post-submit message references IANS Client Services, not faculty
First CS contact window is 24-48 hours
Faculty turnaround presented as separate downstream window
Correction applied across the connector-success message and the close

evals.name: turnaround-windows-always-surfaced
evals.prompt: Set up an AAE call about SOC tooling — no particular deadline.
evals.expected_output: At the Expedite section the skill surfaces faculty turnaround windows for the selected resolution (Faculty Poll 4-6 business days, Phone/Undecided 8-12 business days, expedited prioritized), labeled as downstream after scheduling. First Client Services contact (24-48 hours) is stated separately. Windows render even with no deadline. Post-submit confirmation restates both windows distinctly.
evals.notes: DAAS-184. Reported by Kate Lombard — windows only appeared when Phone was selected, so she couldn't compare expedited vs standard timing.
structural_checklist: Expedite section shows faculty turnaround windows for the selected resolution
24-48 hour CS scheduling window stated separately from faculty turnaround
Windows render whether or not a deadline is set
Post-submit confirmation restates both windows distinctly

evals.name: no-json-artifact-fallback
evals.prompt: [Programmatic test for submit_via_connector.py graceful failure.] Force --mock-tool-not-registered and verify status:connector_unavailable with retry, contact_client_services, and save_scope_draft options.
evals.expected_output: submit_via_connector.py returns status:connector_unavailable, user_message, options array with all three graceful-failure choices, retryable:true. No artifact path referenced.
evals.notes: DAAS-194. Covered by evals/tests/test_submit_via_connector.py.
structural_checklist: status is connector_unavailable
options includes retry, contact_client_services, save_scope_draft
No build_submission_artifact.py invocation
server_error maps to connector_unavailable

evals.name: faculty-poll-phone-nudge
evals.prompt: Set up a Faculty Poll about whether we should adopt zero-trust segmentation, which vendors peers prefer, and how to prioritize identity vs network controls — we need a defensible board position with trade-offs explained.
evals.expected_output: User selects or skill recommends Faculty Poll. check_poll_fit.py flags decision-oriented / multi-topic shape. Skill surfaces the verbatim Phone nudge. User can accept switch to Phone or keep Poll; both paths proceed. If user keeps Poll despite nudge, skill notes the decline internally.
evals.notes: DAAS-196 / DAAS-208. Covered by evals/tests/test_check_poll_fit.py.
structural_checklist: check_poll_fit.py run when Faculty Poll selected
Nudge fires only when heuristic matches
User can accept switch or keep Poll
Nudge wording matches SKILL.md verbatim
Keep-Poll path fully supported

evals.name: post-submit-timing-24-48-hours
evals.prompt: [Post-submit message check after successful connector submit.] Complete an AAE Phone submission and read the confirmation.
evals.expected_output: Confirmation states an IANS Client Services coordinator will contact the user within 24-48 hours to schedule. Faculty turnaround (8-12 business days for Phone) is a separate downstream window — NOT used as the first-contact promise. Does not say 'hear from coordinator within 8-12 business days'.
evals.notes: DAAS-207. Faculty check-in 2026-05-29. CS scheduling is 24-48 hours; 8-12 is faculty turnaround after scheduling.
structural_checklist: First CS contact window is 24-48 hours
Faculty turnaround is separate downstream window
8-12 business days not used as first-contact promise
Step 5 and Step 6 wording aligned
